spec_match.rationale: Correctly probes the HTMLAnchorElement, HTMLImageElement, request initialization objects, and PermissionsPolicy features associated with Attribution Reporting.

interactive.rationale: Highly interactive, providing user controls to run diagnostic probes asynchronously, view dynamic result badges, and study an auto-updating timeline log.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Perfectly aligned with the parent description of verifying attributionsrc attributes, fetch options, and Permissions Policies.

edge_cases.rationale: Includes try-catch blocks to capture fetch execution states and handles environments where Permissions/Feature Policy objects are absent.

references.rationale: Directly references target ChromeStatus entries, specifications, and companion concepts.

design_system.rationale: Perfect styling conformance utilizing standard colors, borders, lists, and typographic styles.

runtime_evidence.rationale: Fresh Chrome DevTools MCP v152 sweep loaded the copied concept, exercised every detected control, found named controls, and reported no post-triage runtime/action failure.

conformance.rationale: A v152 suite now carries the same feature-ID/spec contract without modifying the immutable source-milestone suite.

summary: A stellar interactive diagnostic utility that probes the key network-level and JS-level surfaces of the deprecated Attribution Reporting API. With live badges, asynchronous state tests, and a persistent log terminal, it serves as an exceptional conformance and capability resource.
